{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |Image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] 10 L (China) |Image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |Image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |Image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image=[[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]]

For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周|12=draft}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦|12=draft}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢|12=draft}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=draft}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐|12=draft}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ|12=draft}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=draft}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=draft}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=draft}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]]

After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]]

{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-eight mansions|ja|二十八宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|ja|箏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|volume|ja|体積|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Shakkanhō|ja|尺貫法|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|ja|体積|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Shō|ja|升|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Shō|ja|升|qq=draft|qq=draft}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku (unit)|ja|石 (単位)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|ja|メートル法|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|ja|周|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin dynasty|ja|秦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Wei|ja|魏 (三国)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|ja|唐|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|tin|ja|ブリキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tin|qq=}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|qq=}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|qq=}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|qq=}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|qq=}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Later Han|ja|後漢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|qq=}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|qq=}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|qq=}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|qq=}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]]

After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|qq=}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[sn:To (unit)]] [[ja:斗]] [[sn:o (unit)]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:尺貫法]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]]

{{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|qq=}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|qq=}} ("1-to can"). [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|qq=|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|qq=|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|qq=|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}}

These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}}

After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}}

revision.text:
[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Units of volume]] }} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078168]] Wikidata name: to [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-eight mansions]]|斗宿|The 11th string of a [[:en:Koto (instrument)|Koto (instrument)]]|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of [[:en:Volume|volume]] in the [[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System=[[:en:Japanese units of measurement|Shakkanhō]] (multiple unit) |Quantity=[[:en:Volume|Volume]] |Definition=10 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 [[:en:Sheng (volume)|Shō]] equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 [[:en:Koku|Koku (unit)]]. In Japan, during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the [[:en:Metric system|Metric system]], sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the [[:en:Zhou dynasty|Zhou dynasty]], 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the [[:en:Qin (state)|Qin dynasty]], it was 3.43 liters. - In the [[:en:Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|Later Han]]{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] and [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], it reached 5.94 liters. - By the [[:en:Ming dynasty|Ming dynasty]], it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular [[:en:Tinplate|tin]] can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Twenty-eight mansions}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=Koto (instrument)}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|Volume|en|Volume|WD=Q39297|lt=volume}} in the {{ill|Japanese units of measurement|en|Japanese units of measurement|WD=Q1029751|lt=Shakkanhō}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Japanese units of measurement|en|Japanese units of measurement|WD=Q1029751|lt=Shakkanhō}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|en|Volume|WD=Q39297|lt=Volume}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Sheng (volume)|en|Sheng (volume)|WD=Q995076|lt=Shō}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Sheng (volume)|en|Sheng (volume)|WD=Q995076|lt=Shō}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku (unit)}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|en|Metric system|WD=Q232405|lt=Metric system}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Market System (Chinese)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|en|Zhou dynasty|WD=Q35216|lt=Zhou dynasty}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin (state)|en|Qin (state)|WD=Q34756|lt=Qin dynasty}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|en|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|WD=Q7209|lt=Later Han}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|en|Ming dynasty|WD=Q9903|lt=Ming dynasty}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|Tinplate|en|Tinplate|WD=Q2556927|lt=tin}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=Poly tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix ill template destination links
revision.text:
[[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]][[id:To (satuan ukur)]][[ja:斗]][[ko:말 (단위)]][[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{otheruses|Volume unit in the Shakkanhō system|One of the {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Twenty-eight mansions|1=Twenty-Eight Mansions}}|斗宿|The 11th string of a {{ill|Koto (instrument)|en|Koto (instrument)|WD=Q289037|lt=Koto (instrument)|1=Koto (instrument)}}|筝}} {{Refimprove|date=December 2021}} '''To''' (斗) is a unit of {{ill|Volume|en|Volume|WD=Q39297|lt=volume|1=Volume}} in the {{ill|Japanese units of measurement|en|Japanese units of measurement|WD=Q1029751|lt=Shakkanhō|1=Japanese units of measurement}} system. {{Unit |Name=To (斗) |System={{ill|Japanese units of measurement|en|Japanese units of measurement|WD=Q1029751|lt=Shakkanhō|1=Japanese units of measurement}} (multiple unit) |Quantity={{ill|Volume|en|Volume|WD=Q39297|lt=Volume|1=Volume}} |Definition=10 {{ill|Sheng (volume)|en|Sheng (volume)|WD=Q995076|lt=Shō|1=Sheng (volume)}} |SI=Approx. 18.039 L (Japan)
10 L (China) |image= |Caption=An 18-liter can }} 1 {{ill|Sheng (volume)|en|Sheng (volume)|WD=Q995076|lt=Shō|1=Sheng (volume)}} equals 1/10 of a To, and 10 To equals 1 {{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku (unit)|1=Koku}}. In Japan, during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, 1 shō was standardized to approximately 1.8039 liters, making 1 to approximately 18.039 liters. In China, 1 shō was defined as 1 liter, making 1 to equivalent to 10 liters. Although no longer in practical use in modern China, "斗" was used historically to represent 10 liters (1 decaliter, daL) in the {{ill|Metric system|en|Metric system|WD=Q232405|lt=Metric system|1=Metric system}}, sometimes referred to as "公斗" in Chinese. The {{ill|Market System (Chinese)|ja|市制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market System (Chinese)|1=Q11059428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11059428}} unit "市斗" also corresponds to 10 liters. However, the value of the to varied over different historical periods, generally increasing over time. For example: - During the {{ill|Zhou dynasty|en|Zhou dynasty|WD=Q35216|lt=Zhou dynasty|1=Zhou dynasty}}, 1 to was 1.94 liters. - During the {{ill|Qin (state)|en|Qin (state)|WD=Q34756|lt=Qin dynasty|1=Qin (state)}}, it was 3.43 liters. - In the {{ill|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|en|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|WD=Q7209|lt=Later Han|1=Han dynasty}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}, it was reduced to 1.98 liters. - It increased to 2.02 liters during the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}} and {{ill|Western Jin|ja|西晋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Jin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Jin Dynasty|WD=Q1145470}} dynasties. - During the {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty|1=Sui dynasty}} and {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, it reached 5.94 liters. - By the {{ill|Ming dynasty|en|Ming dynasty|WD=Q9903|lt=Ming dynasty|1=Ming dynasty}}, it grew to 10.74 liters, close to its modern value. In Japan, a rectangular {{ill|Tinplate|en|Tinplate|WD=Q2556927|lt=tin|1=Tinplate}} can with a volume of about 1 to was historically called an {{ill|Itto-kan|ja|一斗缶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itto-kan|1=itto-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352719}} ("1-to can"). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, the name "5-gallon can" (5 USG = 18.93 liters) became widespread, but it was later officially renamed the "18-liter can." {{ill|Plastic|ja|プラスチック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plastic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合成樹脂|1=Synthetic resin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1418040}} containers (commonly known as {{ill|Poly tank|ja|ポリタンク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Poly tank|1=polyethylene tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11339307}}) for holding substances such as kerosene were developed as replacements for itto-kan. These containers typically have a capacity of 1 to (18 liters) or slightly more, such as 20 liters. ==External Links== * [https://www.tan-i-kansan.com/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category/%E6%96%97%E3%81%AE%E5%AE%B9%E7%A9%8D%EF%BD%9C%E5%8D%98%E4%BD%8D%E6%8F%9B%E7%AE%97%E8%A1%A8/ Volume conversion table for To] {{wiktionary}} {{ill|Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja|尺貫法の単位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Units of the Shakkanhō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|斗|version=100014253|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Itto Can 20101107.jpg|150px|A sealed 18-liter can]] [[ja:斗]] [[en:To (unit)]] [[de:To (Einheit)]] [[id:To (satuan ukur)]] [[ko:말 (단위)]] [[zh:斗 (器具)]] {{wikidata link|Q1078168}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shakkanhō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Units of volume]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
